summary: Set 018 Session 1 (single-session): reshaped mid-session from 'migrate dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb to canonical Option B' to 'extend adoption-bootstrap with a Lightweight adoption tier and an organization-design dialog with abstract pattern catalog.' Audit found target repo (dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb) is not a git repository, has no Python venv, no ai_router/, no session-set scaffolding, and runs a custom BATON-based session protocol with the standalone HTML UAT Checklist Editor against a 21-session ledger embedded in CLAUDE.md - the original migration frame did not fit. Operator redirected toward an abstraction-level extension of the orchestrator itself: add Lightweight (L) vs Full (F) adoption-tier choice at new Step 4.5 of docs/adoption-bootstrap.md, with branching guidance (L skips budget Step 5 entirely; F continues to budget); add a coexistence sub-dialog (replace / parallel / index) for projects with their own session protocol; rewrite Step 6 (Plan alignment) to instruct the AI to propose 2-4 cross-cut candidate organizations backed by an explicit abstract pattern catalog (input artifacts, output artifacts, cross-cutting themes, stated objectives, inferred organizational patterns, risk/dependency layers, stakeholder review boundaries) with watch-out failure modes (over-decomposing, forced cuts, slight variants); branch Step 7 (Lightweight checklists are strict subset, no budget.yaml/router-config.yaml/ai_router/) and Step 9 (Lightweight skips budget monitoring + general cost monitoring; new closing note replaces zero-budget reminder for L). Added 10-line callout block at top of docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md's Cost-budgeted verification modes section disambiguating *budget* tier (within-Full-adoption) from *adoption* tier (Lightweight vs Full) - explicit pointer to bootstrap Step 4.5 + clarification that lightweight projects opt out of cross-provider verification by tier choice not by cost-budgeted exception. adoption-bootstrap.md grew 386 -> 461 lines (+19%); ai-led-session-workflow.md grew by ~10 lines. Spec status callout inserted at top of docs/session-sets/018-healthcare-accessdb-migration/spec.md pointing at ai-assignment.md as the actual deliverable. Memory updates: project_consumer_repos.md healthcare-accessdb section rewritten with audit findings + Lightweight-tier-candidate note (drift-status section trimmed to reflect Sets 015/016/017 closure); new project_lightweight_tier_added_to_bootstrap.md captures the Step 4.5/Step 6 changes + vocabulary watch (budget tier vs adoption tier) + the no-version-bump-needed note (prompt points at master, doc edit auto-picked-up). MEMORY.md index updated. Zero changes to dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb (confirmed by inspection - work is owned by the downstream orchestrator launched via the extension's bootstrap prompt at the operator's UAT-pause convenience). Zero extension TypeScript changes - prompt constant in copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt.ts points at master so doc edit is auto-picked-up by next paste-into-fresh-chat; no package.json version bump, no CHANGELOG.md update, no VSIX rebuild. Total spend: $0 metered (set budget set at $5 then raised mid-session to $10 by operator after the reshape; budget unspent). Operator review of ai-assignment.md before execution + read-back of doc edits at validation served as the verification surface; no cross-provider verifier route invoked per spec cost projection ($0 metered) and per memory feedback_ai_router_usage (router restricted to end-of-session verification, deferred). Acceptance criteria from ai-assignment.md all met: Step 4.5 inserted, Step 6 carries the catalog + 2-4 candidates instruction, Steps 7+9 branched, ai-led-session-workflow.md disambiguator added, spec reshape pointer at top, memory entries updated, no healthcare-accessdb changes, no extension TS changes, no version bump. Original migration spec acceptance criteria are explicitly N/A by reshape (recorded in spec.md's reshape callout).
